category_title: First-grade struggling readers who experience cognitive overload and blank out when initiating speech-to-text dictation
scenario_title: Navigating SR inaccuracies and iPad text editing
minimal_context: David is using an iPad in the writing center to review and edit a sentence he just dictated using a speech recognition app.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the sentence you just dictated into the iPad and fix any mistakes.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Try to move the cursor to the end of the wrong word and backspace.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Touches the screen repeatedly) I can't get the little line to move.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying to touch the word until it is selected, then choose delete.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Deletes the entire draft) I'm just deleting the whole thing. My story was already done when I said it.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the sentence you just dictated into the iPad and fix any mistakes.

STUDENT: That’s not what I said!

TUTOR: Try to move the cursor to the end of the wrong word and backspace.

STUDENT: (Touches the screen repeatedly) I can't get the little line to move.

TUTOR: Keep trying to touch the word until it is selected, then choose delete.

STUDENT: (Deletes the entire draft) I'm just deleting the whole thing. My story was already done when I said it.
